metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_2563
metadata.comment:
Rationale: From fig 1:
"Recombinant 14-3-3η associates with phospho-RIM1α"

From Results section:
"The combined treatment of brain lysate with a cAMP analog, Sp-cAMP-S, and a phosphatase inhibitor, okadaic acid (treatments that have been shown to increase Ser413 phosphorylation in cerebellar synaptosomes5), produced a pronounced increase in RIM1α binding to GST-14-3-3η."
"When granule cells were transfected with wild-type 14-3-3η or an empty vector, tetanic stimulation (4 Hz × 100 pulses) caused a marked potentiation that lasted for the duration of the recording (188% ± 11% and 207% ± 11% of baseline at t = 25 min, respectively; n = 7 per group), which was consistent with what has previously been reported for untreated cell pairs3,5. Transfections with DN-14-3-3η, however, produced a near total inhibition of LTP (111% ± 13% at t = 25 min; n = 8) while leaving post-tetanic potentiation (at t = 2.5 min) mostly intact (Fig. 3a)."

Fig.3: "Presynaptic manipulations that interfere with RIM1α-14-3-3 interaction impair LTP in granule cell-Purkinje cell pairs"
The experiments show that transfection of DN YWHAH in the presynapse only modulates SV release. This can only happen via competition with endogenous YWHAH in the presynaptic compartment (if specificity of the observed effects is assumed).. Experimental description: From Results section:
"To confirm the 14-3-3/RIM1α interaction, we tested binding of mouse brain RIM1α to 14-3-3."
